element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Real-time combat system, character control switching
Up to 8 recruitable characters; some automatic, some conditional on story decisions and party size
MP-cost special attacks assigned before combat; more powerful than standard attacks
Ability allocation system for cooking, item creation, equipment crafting
Create stronger equipment than store-bought; 20-item inventory limit per type

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Upon arriving in towns (mid-to-late game emphasis)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional mechanic revealing character backstory; affects gameplay outcomes
Galactic war plot, disease cure investigation, advanced technology integration

typical_relief_triggers: Item creation and equipment upgrades between battles
typical_tension_triggers: Random battle encounters in dungeons and paths
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Exploration-driven with rhythm punctuated by unpredictable random encounters; narrative tension escalates from personal disease-cure quest to galactic conflict; optional character interactions provide emotional pacing breaks

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Private Actions available in towns; optional character recruitment based on party state and story decisions; optional Symbology and ability customization

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to real-time combat mechanics and party management; random encounters teach battle pacing
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Party expansion increases combat complexity; SP customization becomes critical; equipment crafting enables progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Galactic war narrative escalates stakes; advanced Symbology and crafted equipment required; multi-character AI coordination essential
notable_spikes: First random battle encounter (transition from exploration to combat)
Mid-game party recruitment dependencies (conditional character acquisition creates branching difficulty)
Late-game narrative shift to space-faring conflict (thematic and mechanical intensity increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: Alternation between safe town exploration (NPC interaction, Private Actions, item purchasing/crafting) and dangerous dungeon paths (random encounters, real-time combat)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Kratus town and associated dungeon paths

notable_level_patterns.description: Recruitment branches based on prior decisions and party composition; not all 10 characters obtainable in single playthrough; specific party sizes unlock specific recruits
MP resource scarcity during battles (Symbology costs) balanced against item creation and cooking (SP-based sustainability); inventory limit (20 items) forces strategic decisions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Extended dungeon sequences before town access
